patch.value: ADR: dependency-aware actionability for pm next — ready = active leaf with no open blocked_by; reuse compareCriticalItems + shared blocked_by resolver

Decision record for the readiness model behind `pm next` (pm-nj90).

Context: pm has two notions adjacent to 'can I work on this': (a) blocked STATUS (manual, schema role-tagged), surfaced by pm context's blocked_fallback only when zero active items exist; (b) blocked_by metadata (legacy scalar + blocked_by dependency kind) used by close.ts to auto-unblock dependents when a blocker closes. Neither is combined into a single 'what is ready now' query.

Decision: define ACTIONABILITY as a pure function of the corpus:
- A candidate is an item whose status is ACTIVE (in active_statuses: open/in_progress; excludes blocked-status, draft, terminal).
- A blocker of an item is any id from collectBlockedByIds(item) = the scalar blocked_by plus every blocked_by dependency. A blocker is OPEN when the referenced item exists AND is non-terminal; a missing or terminal blocker is treated as resolved (consistent with close.ts auto-unblock, which only clears when the blocker is terminal).
- A candidate is a CONTAINER when it has >=1 non-terminal descendant (parent rollup). Containers are excluded from both ready and blocked: the actionable work is the leaf, so `pm next` always points at concrete leaves and never at an Epic with open children.
- READY = candidate AND leaf (no open descendants) AND no open blockers.
- BLOCKED = candidate AND leaf AND >=1 open blocker (the would-be-ready queue, annotated with blocker id/title/status).
- recommended = the top READY item by compareCriticalItems (in_progress before open, then priority, order, deadline, recency, id) — i.e. resume in-progress work before starting new work.

Consequences: collectBlockedByIds is extracted to src/core/item/actionability.ts and reused by close.ts (single source of truth for blocked_by resolution). compareCriticalItems and collectSubtreeIds are promoted to public exports of context.ts (no logic change) so next.ts reuses the exact ranking + subtree scoping. The downstream 'unblocks' annotation is computed from the reverse blocked_by graph (items whose blocked_by points at the candidate) to explain why finishing it matters. Default-leaf semantics keep epics/features out of the recommendation without a type allowlist (works for any custom schema).

Decision implemented by pm-nj90: dependency-aware actionability (ready = active leaf with no open blocked_by; containers excluded; recommended = top READY by compareCriticalItems, in-progress first). collectBlockedByIds extracted to core/item/actionability.ts as the single source of truth shared with close.ts auto-unblock; compareCriticalItems/collectSubtreeIds promoted to public context.ts exports. Shipped and verified.

Adopted: actionability is a pure corpus function — ready = active leaf with no open blocked_by; containers (open descendants) excluded; recommended = top READY by compareCriticalItems (in-progress first). Implemented in pm-nj90.
A documented, schema-agnostic readiness model reusing the existing blocked_by resolver and criticality comparator, with no type allowlist.

Implemented in core/item/actionability.ts (computeActionabilityReport) and consumed by pm next; collectBlockedByIds shared with close.ts; compareCriticalItems/collectSubtreeIds promoted to public context.ts exports.
